title: AgriBidX
description: Built a full-stack agriculture bidding platform that connects farmers directly with consumers, eliminating middlemen and ensuring fair pricing through a transparent digital marketplace.

caseStudy.problem: Farmers often struggle to get fair prices due to middlemen, while consumers lack direct access to fresh produce at transparent rates.
constraints: Building a transparent and user-friendly bidding workflow
Managing product listings and bid updates efficiently
Ensuring scalability for multiple users and product categories
architecture: React frontend with responsive UI for farmers and consumers
Node/Express backend for product management and bidding logic
MongoDB database for users, products, and bid history
REST APIs for authentication, listings, and bidding workflows
tradeoffs: MERN stack chosen for end-to-end full-stack development
REST APIs used for simplicity and maintainability
Focused first on core bidding and listing features before advanced marketplace modules
results: Transparent product listing and bidding flow created
Improved usability through a clean and scalable web interface

title: Quick Response Coordination System
description: Developed a real-time coordination platform designed to streamline communication, task assignment, and response tracking during emergency or critical situations.

caseStudy.problem: Emergency and critical situations require a fast, organized system to coordinate teams, assign tasks, and track responses in real time.
constraints: Providing a clear and responsive dashboard for multiple users
Maintaining structured task and alert management
architecture: React frontend with dashboard-based UI for coordination workflows
Node/Express backend for alerts, task assignment, and updates
MongoDB database for incidents, users, and response records
REST APIs for task tracking, communication, and status updates
tradeoffs: MERN stack chosen for faster development and unified architecture
Dashboard-first design prioritized for quick decision-making
Focused on task coordination and alert management before adding advanced real-time features
results: Centralized coordination system for alerts and tasks developed
Improved visibility of incidents and response progress
Created a scalable foundation for emergency workflow management

title: LexiVerse
description: A browser-based search engine built for fast dataset search, relevance ranking, and real-time related term discovery.

caseStudy.problem: Searching through massive text datasets efficiently in the browser is challenging, especially when balancing fast load times, low memory usage, and relevant search ranking.
constraints: Handling large datasets entirely on the client side
Providing relevant multi-word search results without backend support
architecture: HTML, CSS, and JavaScript frontend for browser-based interaction
JSON dataset for fast client-side access
Array-based search approach for low memory overhead
Hash Map frequency counting for related term generation
Custom relevance-based sorting for smarter search result ranking
tradeoffs: Initially explored a Suffix Trie for theoretical search speed, but replaced it due to poor real-world loading performance
Flat array structure was chosen for faster load times and practical usability
Client-side architecture prioritized simplicity and speed over backend complexity
results: Built a browser-based search engine capable of handling large datasets efficiently
Implemented intelligent multi-word search with custom relevance ranking
Added real-time related term cloud generation for improved user exploration
Delivered a fast, responsive search experience fully in the browser
